title: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani

seo.title: Classic Butter Chicken Recipe (Murgh Makhani) - Easy!
seo.metaDescription: Master the classic Butter Chicken recipe (Murgh Makhani) with our easy-to-follow guide. Get tender chicken in a rich, creamy tomato sauce. Perfect for an authentic Indian meal!
h2Headings: How to Make Butter Chicken: Step-by-Step
Tips for the Best Butter Chicken
seo.imageAlt: A bowl of classic butter chicken (Murgh Makhani) with rice and naan, garnished with fresh cilantro.
seo.imageCaption: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) – a true Indian delight!

description: Indulge in the rich, creamy flavors of our classic Butter Chicken (Murgh Makhani) – a true Indian delight! This beloved dish features succulent chicken simmered in a velvety tomato-based sauce, enriched with butter and aromatic spices. It's a comforting and satisfying meal perfect for any occasion.

ingredients.item: Boneless, skinless chicken thighs or breasts

ingredients.notes: soaked in warm water for 15 minutes

instructions.title: Marinate the Chicken
instructions.text: In a large bowl, combine chicken pieces with yogurt, 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste, red chili powder, turmeric powder, garam masala, lemon juice, and salt. Mix well to coat the chicken. Cover and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes, or preferably 2-4 hours for best flavor.

instructions.title: Cook the Chicken
instructions.text: Heat 1 tbsp butter in a large pan or skillet over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken in a single layer, working in batches if necessary to avoid overcrowding. Cook for 5-7 minutes, turning occasionally, until browned and slightly cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside.

instructions.title: Prepare the Makhani Sauce Base
instructions.text: In the same pan, melt another 1 tbsp butter over medium heat. Add chopped onion and saute until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add the remaining 1 tbsp ginger garlic paste and cook for 1 minute until fragrant. Stir in the green cardamom pods, cinnamon stick, and bay leaf.

instructions.title: Simmer the Sauce
instructions.text: Pour in the crushed tomatoes, cumin powder, coriander powder, and sugar. Bring to a gentle simmer, then reduce heat to low, cover, and cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, allowing the flavors to meld. Remove the bay leaf, cinnamon stick, and cardamom pods (if you prefer a smoother sauce).

instructions.title: Blend and Finish the Sauce
instructions.text: Carefully transfer the sauce mixture to a blender. Add the soaked cashews and blend until completely smooth. If the sauce is too thick, add a splash of water. Pour the blended sauce back into the pan. Stir in the cooked chicken, heavy cream, and the remaining 2 tbsp butter. Cook for another 5-7 minutes until the chicken is heated through and the sauce thickens slightly.

instructions.title: Serve with Garnish
instructions.text: Stir in the crushed fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi). Taste and adjust salt or sugar if needed. Garnish with fresh chopped cilantro. Serve hot with basmati rice, naan bread, or roti.

tips: For extra tender chicken, marinate overnight. You can also grill or bake the chicken before adding it to the sauce for a smoky flavor.
Do not skip the fenugreek leaves (kasoori methi); they are essential for the authentic butter chicken aroma and flavor.
If you prefer a milder sauce, reduce the amount of red chili powder. For a richer sauce, use full-fat cream and butter.
article: Butter Chicken Classic Murgh Makhani is a beloved Indian Chicken Recipes dish enjoyed across Indian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
